name: Hamiet Bluiett
description: American jazz saxophonist, clarinetist, and composer (1940–2018)
abstract: Hamiet Bluiett (BLUE-et; September 16, 1940 – October 4, 2018) was an American jazz saxophonist, clarinetist, and composer. His primary instrument was the baritone saxophone, and he was considered one of the finest players of this instrument.

concerts.label: An Evening with Charles Mingus and Old Friends
Midnight Performance: Newport Jazz Festival: Sam Rivers Quartet and Orchestra
Kool Jazz Festival: The Young Lions

works.t: What Ever Happened To The Dream Deferred

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Steve Reich and Musicians with World Saxophone Quartet

about_quotes.speaker: Cascadu
about_quotes.evidence: He toured with Hamiet Bluiett and all these guys and everything, and I’m sitting there with the guy. I don’t know him, I don’t know anything about him, I just know that I saw you in my dream
about_quotes.doc_title: Cascadu interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, November 15, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I went out and sat in with different people and met a lot of different people, sitting in with people like Jon Faddis or David Murray, Hamiet Bluiett had a band at the time called the Telepathic Orchestra
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 25, 2002

about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Hamiet Bluiett — >> [INTERVIEWEE] Hamiet Bluiett — anyway — or maybe it was Lester Bowie or something like that.
